source.title: Crane Structural Damage Incident - WD 73 D Platform

event.top_event: Crane boom structural damage due to override of safety device
actions_taken: Instructed all facilities not to use override during normal operations
Initiated plan to remove overrides from cranes
event.summary: On May 27, 2019, a crane incident occurred on Cox Operating's WD 73 D platform during crew change operations. A third-party Crane Operator activated the high angle boom override while booming up, causing the boom to contact the boom stops and resulting in structural damage to the boom heel, boom stops, and boom cables. The Crane Operator did not obtain required supervisory approval before overriding the safety device. Estimated damage exceeded $25,000 (approximately $80,000). No injuries or pollution occurred.
recommendations: Discontinue use of high angle boom override during normal operations
Develop and implement plan to remove overrides from cranes

hazards.name: Crane lifting operations with high angle boom positioning
Override of boom angle safety device

threats.name: Crane Operator activates high angle boom override without supervisory approval
Crane Operator inattention to boom angle during lifting operations
Crew rushing operations due to crew change day time pressure

consequences.name: Structural damage to crane boom heel and boom stops
Loss of crane availability and operational downtime

controls.name: High angle boom kick out safety device
controls.barrier_role: Primary protection to prevent boom over-angle
controls.lod_basis: Automatic hydraulic recirculation halts boom movement at safe angle

supporting_text: The primary high angle boom override allows the boom to continue to operate in spite of the safety design
The Crane Operator pressed the boom override, which allowed the boom to contact the boom stops
The Crane Operator disregarded the sign posted inside the crane cab that states overriding the boom kick out safety device while booming up or raising the load requires a prior supervisory approval

controls.name: Boom stops mechanical barrier
controls.barrier_role: Secondary protection to limit boom angle
controls.lod_basis: Physical mechanical stops at 78 degrees boom angle, approximately 8 inches from limit

supporting_text: The secondary protection is the boom stops
Continuing to boom up once the boom contacts the boom stops bends the boom
While the boom was against the boom stops, the Crane Operator continued to boom up

controls.name: Supervisory approval requirement for override
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unauthorized override activation
controls.lod_basis: Sign posted in crane cab requiring supervisory approval before override use

supporting_text: The Crane Operator disregarded the sign posted inside the crane cab that states overriding the boom kick out safety device while booming up or raising the load requires a prior supervisory approval
The Crane Operator did not request supervisory approval prior to overriding the high angle boom kick out safety device

Moreover, the FabCon Supervisor, Construction Consultant, Person In Charge (PIC), nor Field Foreman gave the Crane Operator permission to override the high angle kick out at any time during the operations

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) with lift specifications
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper planning and communication of lift parameters
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job planning document specifying lift details, boom angles, and designated signalman

supporting_text: The JSA did not specify the details of the lift, such as lift starting point, lift path, boom angles, or the designated Signalman
Inadequate work permits prepared - The JSA did not specify the details of the lift

controls.name: Crane and Rigging policy training and verification
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure operator competence and policy awareness
controls.lod_basis: Operator received policy copy and was questioned throughout week to ensure understanding

supporting_text: The Crane Operator received a copy of Cox's Crane and Rigging policy. Cox questioned him throughout the week to ensure he understood the policy
Although the new Crane Operator had 11 years of industry experience and 6 years of crane operation experience, he was new to the FabCon construction Crew and the platform

controls.name: Use of tag lines and alternative lifting methods
controls.barrier_role: Administrative/operational control to avoid override use
controls.lod_basis: Rope lines and air tugger used to position loads without requiring boom override

supporting_text: To avoid use of the high angle override, rope lines (tally lines) were used to pull small 2X2 basket loads into the + 10 landing by hand. An air tugger pulled in larger 4x8 baskets on the boat landing area
The Crew then used tag lines to pull the basket onto the landing

controls.name: Crane safety device function testing
controls.barrier_role: Detection and verification of safety system operability
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident testing of anti-two-block switches and boom kick out override

supporting_text: Cox function tested the crane's safety devices including the anti-two-block switches for the auxiliary and load line and the high angle boom kick out override. Cox found the devices in working condition

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
